items.title: Transfers PSV: Schaars bevestigt aanstaande transfer
items.summary: VI volgt het transfernieuws bij PSV op de voet, zeker nu de winterse transferwindow in aantocht is. De doelwitten van de Eindhovenaren, duiding van de clubwatcher, of aandacht in buitenlandse media. We verzamelen alles hier.

items.title: PSV verkoopt jonge aanvaller aan Levante
items.summary: Tai Abed neemt na vierenhalf jaar afscheid van PSV. De 21-jarige Israëliër zet zijn loopbaan voort bij Levante, de nummer negentien van La Liga.

items.title: Kostoulas’s brilliant bicycle kick rescues point for Brighton against Bournemouth
items.summary: Just when it seemed another match would be dominated by the dreariness of a debatable video assistant referee decision, a moment of majesty from Charalampos Kostoulas provided a pertinent reminder of the beauty that football can provide. With his side staring at what would have been a controversial defeat, the Greek substitute found himself facing away from the Bournemouth goal in the first minute of injury time. A touch of his chest bounced the ball upwards, before the 18-year-old produced a wonderful bicycle kick to send the Amex Stadium wild. Continue reading...

items.title: Ibrahimovic junior: 'Ik speel bij Ajax omdat ik mezelf ben, niet door iets anders'
items.summary: Maximilian Ibrahimovic maakte maandagavond zijn eerste speelminuten in een Ajax-shirt, met een invalbeurt bij de beloftenploeg tegen Roda JC Kerkrade (2-2). Op vergelijkingen met zijn vader zat de jonge Zweed na afloop duidelijk niet te wachten.

items.title: Kostoulas’ stunning late overhead kick snatches a point for Brighton
items.summary: FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from Brighton’s match against Bournemouth in the Premier League.

items.title: Assist Van Hecke op wondergoal redt punt voor Brighton na discutabele penalty
items.summary: Het Premier League-duel tussen Brighton & Hove Albion en Bournemouth heeft maandagavond geen winnaar opgeleverd. Een heerlijke omhaal van Charalampos Kostoulas redde in blessuretijd een punt voor de thuisploeg.

items.title: ‘A late moment of magic!’ | Kostoulas scores sensational 90th-minute overhead kick!
items.summary: Kostoulas scores sensational 90th minute overhead kick for Brighton in their Premier League game against Bournemouth.

items.title: Taylor maakt bij mislukt thuisdebuut voor Lazio kennis met de klasse van Nico Paz
items.summary: Kenneth Taylor begon zijn avontuur bij Lazio met een uitzege bij Hellas Verona , maar de eerste thuiswedstrijd bij zijn nieuwe club is op een teleurstelling uitgelopen. Como was met ruime cijfers te sterk. Nico Paz scoorde twee keer, maar miste ook een strafschop.

items.title: Man Utd's complicated relationship with Class of 92
items.summary: Manchester United and the class of 92 have a complicated relationship. But is it a problem?

items.title: Why clamor for Man United, Tottenham 'DNA' makes no sense
items.summary: Fans of Man United and Spurs expect a certain style of play, but it's nothing to do with DNA. Nobody complains when their team is winning.

items.title: Why Premier League clubs keep signing each other's players for the same fee
items.summary: Premier League clubs are doing business with each other at an unusually higher rate this season.

items.title: 'Grow up!' - Scholes and Butt continue war of words with Martinez
items.summary: Paul Scholes and Nicky Butt have continued their war of words with Manchester United's Lisandro Martinez, with Butt telling the Argentine to "grow up" over his response to criticism.

items.title: Thomas Frank insists ‘everything normal’ despite turmoil at Tottenham
items.summary: Manager has ‘been feeling the trust’ from club hierarchy Spurs hampered by injury crisis before visit of Dortmund Thomas Frank has insisted the ­Tottenham hierarchy are standing with him in the face of the storm gripping the club. The manager’s job is in the ­balance, his situation precarious after Saturday’s 2-1 home defeat against West Ham. The Spurs support were so incensed by the result and the continuation of the team’s terrible Premier League form – they have won twice in their past 13 league matches – that they demanded Frank be “sacked in the morning”. Continue reading...

items.title: Conte makes subtle dig at Amorim over Højlund
items.summary: Napoli head coach Antonio Conte has appeared to aim a dig at former Manchester United head coach Ruben Amorim.

items.title: Chelsea host Manchester United in Women's FA Cup
items.summary: Holders Chelsea will take on Manchester United in the fifth round of the Women's FA Cup - a repeat of last year's final.

items.title: Monaco's Balogun: Playing Real Madrid 'a dream'
items.summary: United States men's national team and Monaco striker Folarin Balogun told ESPN he will achieve one of his dreams on Tuesday when the Ligue 1 club face Real Madrid at the Bernabéu in the Champions League.

items.title: City confirm $26.8M Guehi signing from Palace
items.summary: Manchester City have announced the signing of England centre-back Marc Guéhi from Crystal Palace on a five-and-a-half-year deal.

items.title: ‘I am at the best club in England’: Guéhi seals £20m move to Manchester City
items.summary: England defender’s contract was due to expire in summer City are without injured Dias, Gvardiol and Stones Marc Guéhi completed a £20m move to Manchester City from Crystal Palace on Monday, with Pep Guardiola describing him as a high-class defender who is the “perfect age” and will provide an immediate boost owing to injuries. The 25-year-old England international signed a five and a-half year contract worth about £300,000 a week after an agreement was reached on Friday. Continue reading...

items.title: Under-pressure Frank 'feeling trust' of Spurs board
items.summary: Tottenham head coach Thomas Frank says lunch with board members is an "extremely good sign" despite pressure mounting on him during an underwhelming season.

items.title: ‘The dream will have to wait’: Moroccan media reacts to Afcon final heartache
items.summary: Walid Regragui faced calls to resign despite stunning record with Atlas Lions after nightmare that prolongs 50-year wait “There will be no second star for Morocco,” read the headline of Le360.ma, referring to the symbol of an Africa Cup of Nations title being sewn on the shirt of the winning team. “The most prestigious African title is at odds with Morocco. The dream and the ambition will have to wait some time,” laments the writer, Abdelkader El-Aine. It is now 50 years since Morocco won their only Afcon. This month, as hosts of the tournament, Morocco had started to dream of a second title. That became a nightmare on Sunday evening in Rabat. Continue reading...
